purpose: Expose the security-sensitive HTTP boundary around the completed merge lifecycle.
success_outcome: Only the authenticated owner can invoke or replay merge, with deterministic safe responses under retries and concurrency.
anti_goals: Do not implement OAuth providers or login UI.
Do not duplicate Store cart CRUD routes.
Do not accept destination or customer identity from the request.

success_checks: Actor-derived ownership and request-shape guards pass.
Completed replay is journal-first and works with a soft-deleted source.
Stable failures and concurrent requests do not leak or duplicate data.
evidence_required: T3 human checkpoint and rollback/recovery evidence.

stop_conditions: Authenticated actor ownership cannot be proven at the route boundary.
Replay would require restoring or reading the consumed source.
A public response must diverge from the linked API contract.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Before T3 closure record exact HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE markers.
